title: Formula column HYPERLINK()
excerpt:

posts.author: Stuart_Spiers (Stuart Spiers)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi,

So I have a formula that returns a text string based on a status, which works fine (it is a simple nested IF statement)
What I’d like is to convert that text result into a clickable hyperlink.

So what I’d really like is the implementation of the HYPERLINK() function in excel.

To be clear the text is dynamically set based on user choice, so the link column isn’t suitable.

Possible?
Probable?
Maybe?

Thanks.

posts.author: andyjim (Andy)
posts.cookedHtml:
Did this ever go anywhere? +1 for this. I’d love a URL builder style column where i can build a URL based on the data in other columns then make that a clickable link. The formula column works for this but i still need to copy + paste the link into the browser.

The above description would work for me.

posts.author: timtim (Tim Wood)
posts.cookedHtml: +1 for me as well. Did this ever go anywhere??

posts.author: inzeos (Leif Hardison)
posts.cookedHtml: Would also like this feature. Would be very useful to link backed ticket/request type numbers to their actual platforms.

posts.author: JCorrell (Jim - The Monday Man)
posts.cookedHtml:
I agree this would be very nice to have.

For some, one possibility would be using right-click on the formula cell to copy the link. Both Edge and Chrome will then allow you to right-click in the address bar to paste and go to the link. Edge allows you to use Ctrl+Shift+L as well.

Jim - Subscribe to The Monday Man
Watch Our Latest Video: A Killer Combo: monday.com, Integromat & Google Sheets - Recurring Tasks Example

posts.author: AndreSpivo (Andre Bellerive)
posts.cookedHtml: Not having this feature makes me want to go back to gsheets!

posts.author: nick (Nick)
posts.cookedHtml: Would like this too. If you can’t achieve this, then at least allow the Link column type to reference the value in the formula column for the link URL.

posts.author: MFIV (Martin Fivelsdal)
posts.cookedHtml:
Is there any progress on this matter?

It would be very helpful to have this capability!

posts.author: MondayMaster (Ken Snyder)
posts.cookedHtml:
I was able to get around this limitation, but I can’t say that it will work for everyone’s use case.

My workaround was to use an automation to post an UPDATE to the item under certain conditions. The text of the update includes a dynamically constructed URL that does show in the update as a Hyperlink.

This workaround also helped me overcome a challenge with Zendesk integration that I was encountering.

Unfortunately, it doesn’t put the link in the table, but it does put it in the item Update which may result in notifications going out to item subscribers. It’s a workable solution for us until more flexibility arrives in Monday.com.

Screen Shot 2022-03-03 at 9.38.24 AM2534×744 70.3 KB

posts.author: NovelNexus (Aaron Desmond)
posts.cookedHtml: This would be really useful. Any progress or new workarounds?

posts.author: danperks (Dan perks)
posts.cookedHtml: I also could really do with this, not yet found a workaround

posts.author: tara (tara)
posts.cookedHtml: This could be really useful. Just spoke with someone who wants to link to the item id in a mirror column but that it would actually be clickable to the item. The thought was to use a formula of the board and then add the itemID but then it needs to be clickable…

posts.author: Michael_H (Michael_H)
posts.cookedHtml: +1 from me. I simply like to create a clickable link using a formula and two columns, one for a Link to the shipper website and the other a text, providing the tracking number for a shipment. Something like this: HYPERLINK({Link_URL_Column}, {Text_Tracking_No_Column})

+1 from me too.

We need to generate tracking links that can be inserted into an email which is sent to the customer.

The send function needs to be as simple as possible so that our volunteer team can focus on the end user, not on the tech.

posts.author: kkovriga (Katerina Kovriga)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi guys,

Our company Stiltsoft develops the Smart Spreadsheet for monday app - hope it will help you to use Excel-like features right on monday.com platform.

You’ll be able to import/export your data (and boards with items as well) and work with it as a fully functional spreadsheet (filtration, cell formulas, conditional formatting, pivot tables, charts, etc.) in one place.

Best regards,
Katerina Kovriga

posts.author: eClaireS (Claire Sowden)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @kkovriga ,
Can you please advise if this will enable us to import addresses to the location column?
Best,
Claire

posts.author: kkovriga (Katerina Kovriga)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @eClaireS ,
You’ll be able to use plenty of Excel-like functions inside the Smart Spreadsheet. Here you may find the list of available functions to use.
If you have an already created xlsx/csv file, you’ll be able to import it to monday.com and continue working with your data inside the Smart Spreadsheet.

Best regards,
Katerina Kovriga

posts.author: PabloOrtiz (Pablo)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello all, just have dicoverded that if you add a “http://” using the function concatenate on the formula column, monday will automatically add the link to the result of the formula… hope this help you guys, for us it has.

Example, a link for whatsapp

CONCATENATE(“http://WA.ME/”,{Teléfono Móvil})

posts.author: SaintPeter (Rex Schrader)
posts.cookedHtml: Using CONCATENATE worked for me. The resulting URL is not very pretty, but clicking it does open up a calculated URL.

posts.author: GCavin (Gilles Cavin - Reinventing Formulas in monday.com)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Rex,

I know you are, like all of us, looking for a native solution, but in the meantime, if you want a complete solution through a 3rd party app, the Advanced Formula Booster handles this without problem as it can write to any column.

Here is the documentation related to the Link column.
